data.content:
Implementation plan for interactive Copilot session support

Current code shape
- `src/lib/agent-session-capabilities.ts` declares Copilot as one-shot (`interactive=false`, `cli-arg`, `never-opened`) while only Codex gets an interactive preset.
- `src/lib/terminal-manager-initial-child.ts` and `src/lib/terminal-manager-take-child.ts` hard-code interactive preference to Codex, which keeps Copilot on the prompt-mode path for claimed-step and take-loop sessions.
- `src/lib/agent-session-runtime.ts` already centralizes stdin lifecycle, follow-up handling, AskUser auto-response, watchdogs, and process-group abort/timeout behavior. The Copilot work should reuse this instead of bespoke logic.
- `src/lib/agent-adapter.ts` already normalizes Copilot JSON events into the shared semantic model, but the normalizer is still framed as mapping into Claude-shaped output and does not yet sit behind an interactive Copilot transport/session entrypoint.

Planned implementation steps
1. Introduce Copilot interactive/session capabilities.
   Files: `src/lib/agent-session-capabilities.ts`, `src/lib/agent-adapter.ts`, `src/lib/terminal-manager-initial-child.ts`, `src/lib/terminal-manager-take-child.ts`.
   Work: add an explicit Copilot interactive capability preset, select it for claimed-step and take-loop session creation, and add a stable Copilot interactive spawn path that preserves existing `--model` selection support. Remove `dialect === \"codex\"` assumptions from the session-start decision so transport is capability-driven.
2. Generalize the interactive transport/session wrapper.
   Files: `src/lib/codex-jsonrpc-session.ts` or a renamed/reworked shared transport wrapper plus the callers above.
   Work: either generalize the current transport abstraction or add a second session client behind the same runtime contract so Copilot can keep follow-up turns in the same process. The runtime should depend on `promptTransport` and shared hooks, not on Codex- or Claude-specific branching.
3. Normalize Copilot lifecycle signals into the shared event model.
   Files: `src/lib/agent-adapter.ts`, any new transport translator, and `src/lib/agent-session-runtime.ts` if small runtime hooks are required.
   Work: ensure Copilot emits explicit normalized `turn_result` from protocol completion signals and cleanly distinguishes that from process exit/session end. Keep AskUser/tool-use normalization intact, ensure duplicate text suppression still works, and align close-input scheduling with the shared runtime contract.
4. Route Copilot claimed-step and take-loop sessions through the shared runtime.
   Files: `src/lib/terminal-manager-initial-io.ts`, `src/lib/terminal-manager-initial-child.ts`, `src/lib/terminal-manager-take-child.ts`, `src/lib/terminal-manager.ts` if abort wiring needs adjustment.
   Work: keep stdin open across follow-up prompts in the same session, then use the shared `close-after-result` drain path. Ensure watchdog timeout, process-group kill, retry handoff, and abort behavior match the existing interactive-agent semantics instead of the current Copilot prompt-mode behavior.
5. Extend fixtures, tests, and protocol docs.
   Files: `scripts/test-fixtures/fake-copilot.sh`, `src/lib/__tests__/agent-session-capabilities.test.ts`, `src/lib/__tests__/agent-adapter.test.ts`, `src/lib/__tests__/agent-session-runtime.test.ts`, `src/lib/__tests__/agent-session-runtime-lifecycle.test.ts`, and targeted terminal-manager take-loop tests; update `docs/interactive-agent-session-protocol.md` and `README.md` if behavior/user-facing support text changes.
   Work: add Copilot interactive coverage for successful completion, hang timeout/watchdog handling, retry/abort behavior, and any new transport translation events.

Child knot hierarchy
- `foolery-9493` Refactor shared interactive runtime for Copilot transport
- `foolery-5f6b` Normalize Copilot interactive events and lifecycle signals
- `foolery-29fa` Add Copilot interactive session regression coverage

Complexity and risks
- Complexity: medium-high. This touches the runtime seam between capability selection, child-process startup, event transport, and retry classification.
- Main risk: choosing the wrong Copilot session entrypoint or assuming Codex-style handshake semantics. Keep the transport isolated behind a small interface so the runtime remains protocol-agnostic.
- Secondary risk: regressing existing one-shot Copilot flows or model-selection behavior. Preserve current model flag support and limit the interactive change to shared session paths.
- Validation risk: false success if turn completion is inferred from stream silence or process exit. The implementation must continue to rely on explicit protocol completion plus existing post-exit workflow validation.

Test strategy
- Unit-test capability resolution and spawn-arg construction for interactive Copilot while preserving model selection.
- Unit-test Copilot event normalization for streamed text, tool calls, AskUser prompts, explicit turn completion, and error completion.
- Runtime tests: successful interactive follow-up flow, watchdog timeout leading to process-group termination, and stdin close behavior after result when no follow-up is sent.
- Terminal-manager tests: Copilot take-loop success path, non-zero/timeout retry path, and abort path to ensure retry does not continue after manual abort.
